theory.canonical_statement: The torsion of the Trawinistic connection vanishes away from the zero point and is allowed only as a finite, localized residue concentrated at the anchor.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This forces the regular manifold to behave like an ordinary torsion-free geometry.
This replaces distributed torsion with a finite localized residue around the anchor.
This closes the progression by requiring the same finite residue for every enclosing neighborhood, which is the signature of point localization.

external_sources.title: Constraint: TZP Torsion Localisation
external_sources.canonical_claim: The torsion of the Trawinistic connection vanishes away from the zero point and is allowed only as a finite, localized residue concentrated at the anchor.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Levi‑Civita connection) supports the theorem analogue (torsion) by showing that Because the Levi‑Civita connection is torsion‑free, torsion vanishes on a manifold endowed with this connection.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: Because the Levi‑Civita connection is torsion‑free, torsion vanishes on a manifold endowed with this connection. This supports the constraint that torsion is localized at the zero point and vanishes elsewhere.
